oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Update on Scarlet Macaw Project
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Leon, Rodrigo
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 50 Number 2 (April 2023).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Update on the Scarlet Macaw ProjectIt has been a while since we posted any news about the recovery project for the Scarlet macaw in the Lacandon Jungle. Below is a summary of the latest events from the 2022 and 2023 seasons.To date there is no information as to the whereabouts of the chicks. All the young birds were leg banded with steel rings, and had microchips implanted as well. A few of the local people did come forth and speculated that the chicks were sold to nearby ranches since there was no evidence they were sold in Guatemala (a main route of departure of macaws) or to the Mexican cities of Comitán or Palenque.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 50 No. 2 (2023): Watchbird April 2023; 20-21
